feed.title: SAP Community - ABAP Development
feed.subtitle: ABAP Development blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: Claude ported ABAP functions to new API
entry.content:
I have recently shared an article with my objections to using git as a remote file system to get access to ABAP source code. In the article I pushed the idea of using git for versioning and using other tools for getting access to the source code - either MCP or something else (VS Code extension, command-line tool).

Today I want to share a short story backing my claims.

In our ABAP development process, we do change code in private or rather project-related systems. Yeah, sounds crazy expensive but we do that — you can save a lot of money if you do not keep the systems running 24/7 but 8/5 (also these systems are used by 1 up to 10 developers, testers, POs, etc., so they do not need the officially recommended resources — they work with half of the resources).

When the change is ready, developers commit + push to a remote git repository, open a pull request, a CI job starts, runs tools like abaplint or abap-cleaner and then deploys a fresh new ABAP system, clones the related repository into the new system, runs ABAP Units and ATCs and so on.

I hope the above explains our need to control abapGit remotely (these days, we primarily use gCTS which has its officially supported REST API but our CI pipelines are much older and abapGit is supported too). To control abapGit remotely, we developed RFC function modules which are available at

https://github.com/abapGit/abapgit-api-rfc

If you are wondering why RFC function modules and not something else, it is because it is super simple to install them with abapGit itself.

When we set up a new ABAP system with abapGit, we do install the development version, so we need to clone the remote repo and push it to the target system using our helper tool called sapcli:

git clone https://github.com/abapgit/abapgit
cd abapgit
sapcli checkin package --starting-folder src '$abapgit'

Then we need to push the abapgit-api-rfc:

git clone https://github.com/abapgit/abapgit-api-rfc
cd abapgit-api-rfc
sapcli checkin package --starting-folder src '$abapgit_api_rfc'

Here the fun part starts. That process usually fails because sapcli is often behind the abapGit format or the abapgit-api-rfc is behind abapGit interfaces and everything is behind the ABAP runtime.

Note: we haven't been using the abapgit-api-rfc for a while because of gCTS but some developers insist on using abapGit for exporting source code for agents and thus we revived our efforts with this project.

After I published my do-not-abuse-git post, I went to prepare a new system, but since I was expecting problems, I did want AI to fix them for me, so I asked Claude to install:

Please install this project written in ABAP and serialized by abapGit into my ABAP system. Use the command line tool sapcli too. The sapcli environment vars for connection are already exported in the environment. Run: sapcli checkin --starting-folder src '$abapgit_api_rfc'

By the way, I provided Claude with an invalid command but it ran --help and corrected it itself.

After successful creation and writing of the related objects, sapcli tried to activate them but the activation failed with two types of errors:

- Type BAPIRET2 is not a table type
- Method "SET" is unknown or PROTECTED or PRIVATE

Of course, Claude automatically analyzed the files of the repository and informed me about the status:

● The objects were created on the system but activation failed with two types of errors:

  Error 1: Type BAPIRET2 is not a table type (all 4 function modules)
  All function modules use TABLES RETURN STRUCTURE BAPIRET2. The target system doesn't accept BAPIRET2 (a structure) in TABLES parameters for RFC-enabled function modules. It likely needs a table type like BAPIRET2_T or BAPIRET2_TAB.

  Error 2: Method "SET" is unknown or PROTECTED or PRIVATE (LINK, PULL, SWITCHBRANCH) 
  The code calls zcl_abapgit_login_manager=>set(...) but this method doesn't exist (or isn't public) in the abapGit version installed on your system. The API may have changed.

  Would you like me to:

  1. Fix the BAPIRET2 issue by changing TABLES parameters to use a proper table type?

  2. Investigate the set method issue — do you know what version of abapGit is on the target system, or the correct method name for setting credentials?

Finally! We are getting to the point where Claude fetched the corresponding sources from the system itself.

My next prompt was:

Investigate the set method issue. You can use sapcli to get abapGit source code. For example "sapcli class read zcl_abapgit_login_manager".

If you ask why I didn’t just tell Claude the abapGit repo is in the directory ../abapgit - it is not usually the case that you have all the dependencies up to date and handy on your machine and you probably also want to check the sources on the system because who can assure that the git repo contains the same version as the one on the system?

It took Claude 30 seconds to read the class, find out the new method name, patch abapgit-api-rfc and re-run sapcli checkin to verify the fix was working.

● Bash(sapcli class read zcl_abapgit_login_manager)                                  
  ⎿  CLASS zcl_abapgit_login_manager DEFINITION                                      
       PUBLIC                                                                        
       FINAL                                                                         
     … +49 lines (ctrl+o to expand)                                                  
                                                                                     
● The old set method has been renamed to set_basic in the installed version. I'll update the three function modules that call it.                                    
                                                                                     
● Read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)                                                     
                                                                                     
● Now I'll replace set with set_basic in all three files.

Then Claude also asked if I wanted to fix the problem with BAPIRET2. However, since I am not primarily an ABAP developer, I had to ask it to read ABAP syntax documentation. Then Claude took off and I have no words to describe how impressive the experience was, just read the following conversation:

● Now, shall I investigate and fix the BAPIRET2 table type issue?

❯ Yes, but check ABAP syntax documentation.

● Web Search("ABAP function module TABLES parameter RFC "table type" DBSTRUCT syntax documentation site:help.sap.com")

● Fetch(https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_752_index_htm/7.52/en-us/abaptables_parameters_obsolete.htm)

● Fetch(https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_752_index_htm/7.52/en-US/abenrfc_limitations.htm)

● The documentation confirms TABLES parameters accept both TYPE itab_type (table type) and STRUCTURE struct (structure). The current code uses STRUCTURE BAPIRET2 which should be valid. But the error "Type BAPIRET2 is not a table type" in context "RFC/UPDATE" suggests something specific about this system.

  Let me check what BAPIRET2 looks like on the target system and if a table type exists.

● Bash(sapcli --help 2>&1 | head -40)
  ⎿  usage: sapcli [-h] [--version] [-v] [--ashost ASHOST] [--sysnr 
     … +16 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

● Bash(sapcli structure read BAPIRET2 2>&1)
  ⎿  @EndUserText.label : 'Returnparameter'
     @AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
     define structure bapiret2 {
     … +27 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

● Bash(sapcli dataelement read BAPIRET2 2>&1 || true)
  ⎿  Exception (SAPCliError):
       Object {type} does not support plain 'text' format

● Bash(sapcli table read BAPIRET2_T 2>&1 || sapcli structure read BAPIRET2_T 2>&1 || echo "BAPIRET2_T not found")
  ⎿  Exception (ExceptionResourceNotFound):
       Inactive version for TABL BAPIRET2_T does not exist
     Exception (ExceptionResourceNotFound):
     … +2 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

● Bash(sapcli table read BAPIRET2_TAB 2>&1 || echo "BAPIRET2_TAB not found")
  ⎿  Exception (ExceptionResourceNotFound):
       Inactive version for TABL BAPIRET2_TAB does not exist
     BAPIRET2_TAB not found

● Bash(sapcli datapreview --help 2>&1)
  ⎿  usage: sapcli datapreview [-h] {osql} ...

     positional arguments:
     … +5 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

● Bash(sapcli datapreview osql -q "SELECT TYPENAME FROM DD40L WHERE ROWTYPE = 'BAPIRET2'" 2>&1)
  ⎿  Error: Exit code 2
      ...
     sapcli: error: unrecognized arguments: -q

● Bash(sapcli datapreview osql "SELECT TYPENAME FROM DD40L WHERE ROWTYPE =
      'BAPIRET2'" 2>&1)
  ⎿  TYPENAME
     /BA1/F4_TAB_MESSAGE
     /BA1/F4_TAB_MSG
     … +51 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

● BAPIRET2_T exists on the system. Now I understand the full picture:

  - The ABAP documentation says TABLES parameters accept either TYPE itab_type (tabletype) or STRUCTURE struc (structure)
  - The current XML uses <DBSTRUCT>BAPIRET2</DBSTRUCT> which maps to STRUCTURE BAPIRET2
  - But for RFC-enabled function modules on this system, the TABLES parameter requires a table type, not a structure
  - BAPIRET2_T is available as a standard table type with BAPIRET2 row type I need to change the XML from <DBSTRUCT>BAPIRET2</DBSTRUCT> to <DBSTRUCT>BAPIRET2_T</DBSTRUCT> — but wait, that's still the STRUCTURE tag. Let me check how abapGit serializes a TYPE-based TABLES parameter. The correct XML tag
  should be <TYP> instead of <DBSTRUCT>.

Claude read the help of sapcli to check if the tool provides the features it needs to investigate the problem, then it used sapcli to check the existing types on the system and since sapcli does not support searching for a table type by its line type yet, Claude fell back to a brute-force approach and used sapcli’s datapreview to read ABAP types directly from the database to find the right type for the return parameter.

However, in the first attempt Claude modified the XML managed by abapGit itself. Claude did the correct modification (from DBSTRUCT to TYP) but sapcli could not process it, Claude fell back to DBSTRUCT with the new type BAPIRET2_T.

I was not sure what would happen if I tried to pull that change via abapGit into the system because BAPIRET2_T is not a table. So I reverted the change, pushed the modified function module into the system without the DBSTRUCT change (because sapcli only checks the XML but uses the source codes only) and then I checked the delta in abapGit directly. In the end, it turned out that the correct format really is <TYP>BAPIRET2_T</TYP> and not <DBSTRUCT>BAPIRET2_T</DBSTRUCT>. However, Claude would happily commit the wrong format and then a human doing git pull would probably get an error in a better case or the function would be doing something else than expected — I do not know.

Here my short story ends. I know the fixes were trivial but my intention was to show why I do not want to rely solely on git as a vehicle to deliver ABAP source code for agents. I think you can also see why I do not really use MCPs much — I create tools and scripts that can be easily enriched with the needed logic to handle specific repetitive tasks and can be reused in CI pipelines or other automations (it is like building Lego — you just need to create your own bricks too).

PS: the pull request is still under review: https://github.com/abapGit/abapgit-api-rfc/pull/8

entry.title: SAP BTP ABAP Environment Customer & Partner Roundtable – April 07, 2026
entry.content:
Dear customers, partners, and SAP Community members,

Join us for the next session of our SAP BTP ABAP Environment Customer & Partner Roundtable, taking place on April 07, 2026, from 10:00–11:00 CET.

Our roundtable is a monthly virtual meeting where we share the latest updates from the product team and showcase live demos from experts. It is a great opportunity to stay up to date with the latest capabilities of the SAP BTP ABAP Environment, ask questions, and engage with the community.

Join the session

📅Date: April 07, 2026
⏰Time: 10:00–11:00 CET

Agenda

🔹Product Updates – Frank Jentsch

🔹App as an Entity – Aron Leibfried

🔹Direct App Launch – Aparna Narayanaswamy

The roundtable is a monthly series taking place on the first Tuesday of every month (10:00–11:00 CET).

👉 Download the .ics calendar file for the full 2026 series and import it directly into your calendar.
Each calendar entry already includes the Microsoft Teams meeting link.

Microsoft Teams meeting link (valid for the entire 2026 series): Microsoft Teams meeting link 

All agendas, slides, recordings, and updates from past and current sessions are published centrally via the
SAP Community – SAP BTP ABAP Environment Customer & Partner Roundtable.

We’re looking forward to seeing you there!

SAP BTP ABAP Environment Team

entry.title: How to create a RAP application using a parameterized CDS view
entry.content:
Hello everyone, recently I came across a somewhat unusual requirement where I needed to use a parameter in a RAP application. As we know, this isn’t natively supported with Fiori Elements, so I had to look for a way to make it work. After some research and a few tests, I ended up with a solution that I consider quite innovative: using a Fiori Freestyle approach.

The best part is that it requires only a minimal amount of code, something that any ABAP developer can implement without much effort, and it becomes extremely useful whenever you need to work with parameters that are not directly linked to the data exposed by the CDS view.

The idea behind this solution is simple:

Since projection views in RAP do not support CDS parameters directly, we expose a parameterized interface CDS view through a service definition and service binding.

Then, using a Fiori Freestyle application with a Smart Table, we dynamically inject the parameter into the table binding path before the data request is sent to the backend.

This allows the RAP service to consume the parameterized CDS view without requiring complex workarounds.

 

Let’s go through the required steps:

 

1)  Since it’s not possible to add WITH PARAMETERS to a projection view, we need to create an interface CDS view and use it as our “projection.”

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: '<your cds description>'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: false
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true

define view entity <your_parameterized_interface_cds>
  with parameters
    p_date : abap.char(8)

as select from <your_source_cds_or_table>
(
  p_date : $parameters.p_date
)
{
    key <your_key_field>,
        <your_field_1>,
        <your_field_2>,
        <your_field_3>
}

2) Create our service definition normally

3) Create Service Binding ( Odata V2 )

4) Now we have to create the freestyle app with the fiori generator application

5) Now just connect to the S4 and select the service binding created earlier

At this point, you can see the project structure in the Explorer panel on the left. We’ll focus only on the view and controller folders.

 

 

 

6)

First, we need to create our Smart Table in the view folder. Inside the SmartFilterBar, we must set the entitySet from our service binding. After that, we add a customControl in my case, I used a DatePicker, but you can use any control that fits your requirement. I also attached an onDateChange event to the DatePicker so we can capture and manipulate the selected date whenever it changes.

Within the Smart Table, we also need to define the same entitySet. Another very important point here is the beforeRebindTable event. This is the hook that allows us to pass the parameter to the CDS view. It will trigger the onBeforeRebindTable method in the controller, where we can dynamically inject the parameter into the binding before the data is requested.

 

<mvc:View
    xmlns:table="sap.ui.table"
    xmlns="sap.ui.comp.smartfilterbar"
    xmlns:f="sap.f"
    xmlns:m="sap.m"
    xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc"
    xmlns:smartTable="sap.ui.comp.smarttable"
    controllerName="<your.app.namespace>.controller.Main"
    height="100%">

<f:DynamicPage
    id="dynamicPage"
    fitContent="true"
    title="{i18n>appTitle}">

<f:header>
<f:DynamicPageHeader>

<SmartFilterBar
    id="smartFilterBar"
    entitySet="<your_entity_set>">

    <controlConfiguration>

        <ControlConfiguration
            key="<your_parameter_field>"
            visibleInAdvancedArea="true"
            label="<your_filter_label>"
            mandatory="true">

            <customControl>

                <m:DatePicker
                    id="datePicker"
                    displayFormat="dd/MM/yyyy"
                    valueFormat="yyyyMMdd"
                    placeholder="dd/mm/yyyy"
                    width="100%"
                    required="true"
                    change="onDateChange"/>

            </customControl>

        </ControlConfiguration>

    </controlConfiguration>

</SmartFilterBar>

</f:DynamicPageHeader>
</f:header>

<f:content>

<smartTable:SmartTable
    id="smartTable"
    beforeRebindTable="onBeforeRebindTable"
    showRowCount="true"
    enableAutoBinding="false"
    entitySet="<your_entity_set>"
    smartFilterId="smartFilterBar"
    useVariantManagement="true"
    tableType="Table"
    initiallyVisibleFields="<your_fields>"
    enableAutoColumnWidth="true"
    showTablePersonalisation="true">

    <smartTable:customToolbar>

        <m:OverflowToolbar design="Transparent">
            <m:ToolbarSpacer/>
        </m:OverflowToolbar>

    </smartTable:customToolbar>

</smartTable:SmartTable>

</f:content>

</f:DynamicPage>

</mvc:View>

7) In this step, we implement the logic inside the onBeforeRebindTable method, which is responsible for dynamically passing the parameter to the CDS view before the Smart Table triggers the data request.

First, the code retrieves the value entered in the DatePicker using byId. This value will be used as the parameter for the CDS. A local variable (sDate) is initialized to store the selected date.

Next, a validation is performed to ensure that the date field is filled in. Since this parameter is mandatory for the query, if no value is provided the DatePicker is set to the Error state and a validation message is displayed. At the same time, the table binding is stopped using:

oBindingParams.preventTableBind = true;

This prevents the Smart Table from calling the backend without the required parameter, avoiding unnecessary requests and possible errors.

If the date is correctly filled in, any previous error state is cleared by setting the ValueState back to None.

Finally, the most important part happens: the table binding path is updated dynamically. Here we pass the selected date to the parameterized CDS by changing the binding path of the Smart Table:

setTableBindingPath("/<EntitySet>(p_date='${sDate}')/Set")

This is what allows the RAP application to consume a CDS with parameters, because the parameter is injected at runtime just before the data is requested from the backend.

onBeforeRebindTable: function (oEvent) {

    let oDatePicker = this.byId("datePicker");
    let sDate = "";

    if (oDatePicker) {
        sDate = oDatePicker.getValue();
    }

    if (!sDate) {

        if (oDatePicker) {
            oDatePicker.setValueState("Error");
            oDatePicker.setValueStateText("Please inform the date.");
        }

        let oBindingParams = oEvent.getParameter("bindingParams");

        if (oBindingParams) {
            oBindingParams.preventTableBind = true;
        }

        return;
    }

    if (oDatePicker) {
        oDatePicker.setValueState("None");
    }

    this.getView()
        .byId("smartTable")
        .setTableBindingPath("/<your_entity_set>(p_date='" + sDate + "')/Set");

}

And Vooooalá!

Conclusion

Although RAP and Fiori Elements provide powerful tools for building enterprise applications, there are still scenarios where certain features are not directly supported, such as consuming parameterized CDS views in projection layers.

By combining RAP services with a lightweight Fiori Freestyle approach, we can overcome this limitation in a simple and flexible way. Using events like beforeRebindTable and dynamically modifying the binding path allows parameters to be passed to the CDS view at runtime.

This approach requires only a small amount of additional UI code and can be implemented quickly by ABAP developers who are already familiar with RAP and Fiori.

I hope this solution helps others facing similar challenges when working with parameterized CDS views in RAP applications.

entry.title: Handling Multi-Source Data Using Custom Entities in Unmanaged RAP
entry.content:
Introduction

In modern SAP application development using the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model, developers often encounter scenarios where data must be retrieved from multiple sources instead of a single database table.

For such cases, Custom Entities provide a flexible solution. They allow developers to implement custom query logic using ABAP classes while still leveraging RAP features such as associations, navigation, and UI annotations.

In this blog, we will implement a Travel–Booking scenario using Custom Entities where data is fetched dynamically through a query implementation class.

Create Query Implementation Class

First, create a class that implements the RAP interface:

INTERFACE if_rap_query_provider

This interface allows developers to implement custom query logic.

CLASS zcl_custom_entitys DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC.

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    " Interface used for implementing query logic for Custom Entities
    INTERFACES if_rap_query_provider.

ENDCLASS.


CLASS zcl_custom_entitys IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD if_rap_query_provider~select.

    " Variables used for paging, entity identification and filtering
    DATA: lv_top        TYPE i,
          lv_offset     TYPE i,
          lv_entity     TYPE string,
          lv_conditions TYPE string,
          lv_total      TYPE int8.

    " Read paging parameters from OData request
    lv_top    = io_request->get_paging( )->get_page_size( ).
    lv_offset = io_request->get_paging( )->get_offset( ).

    " Identify which custom entity is being queried
    lv_entity = io_request->get_entity_id( ).

    " Ensure page size is valid
    IF lv_top < 0.
      lv_top = 1.
    ENDIF.

    " Retrieve filter conditions from request
    lv_conditions = io_request->get_filter( )->get_as_sql_string( ).

    " Handle Travel Custom Entity request
    IF lv_entity = 'ZI_TRAVEL_CUSTOMENTITY'.

      " Fetch data from travel table with paging and filter conditions
      SELECT travel_id,
             agency_id,
             customer_id,
             begin_date,
             end_date,
             booking_fee,
             total_price,
             currency_code,
             description,
             CASE
               WHEN overall_status = 'O' THEN 'Open'
               WHEN overall_status = 'A' THEN 'Accepted'
               WHEN overall_status = 'X' THEN 'Cancel'
             END AS overall_status
        FROM /dmo/travel_m
        WHERE (lv_conditions)
        ORDER BY travel_id, agency_id
        INTO TABLE (lt_travel)
        OFFSET _offset
        UP TO _top ROWS.

      " Calculate number of records returned
      lv_total = lines( lt_travel ).

      " Send response back to RAP framework
      io_response->set_data( lt_travel ).
      io_response->set_total_number_of_records( lv_total ).

    ENDIF.

  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

This class dynamically fetches data using paging parameters such as $top and $skip.

Define Root Custom Entity (Travel)

Next, define the root custom entity in CDS.

@EndUserText.label: 'Travel Custom Entity'
@ObjectModel: {
  query.implementedBy: 'ABAP:ZCL_CUSTOM_ENTITYS'
}
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true

define root custom entity Zi_travel_customentity
{

  key Travel_id : /dmo/travel_id;
  Agency_id     : /dmo/agency_id;
  Customer_id   : /dmo/customer_id;
  Begin_date    : /dmo/begin_date;
  End_date      : /dmo/end_date;

  @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'currency_code'
  booking_fee : /dmo/booking_fee;

  @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'currency_code'
  Total_price : /dmo/total_price;

  currency_code : /dmo/currency_code;
  description   : /dmo/description;

  overall_status : abap.char(10);

  _Booking : composition [1..*] of ZI_BOOKING_CUSTOMENTITY;

}

Define Item Custom Entity (Booking)

Now create the booking entity and associate it with Travel.

@EndUserText.label: 'Booking Entity'
@ObjectModel : {
  query.implementedBy: 'ABAP:ZCL_CUSTOM_ENTITYS'
}
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true

define custom entity ZI_BOOKING_CUSTOMENTITY
{

  key travel_id  : /dmo/travel_id;
  key booking_id : /dmo/booking_id;

  booking_date : /dmo/booking_date;
  customer_id  : /dmo/customer_id;
  carrier_id   : /dmo/carrier_id;
  connection_id: /dmo/connection_id;
  flight_date  : /dmo/flight_date;

  @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'currency_code'
  flight_price : /dmo/flight_price;

  currency_code : /dmo/currency_code;
  booking_status: /dmo/booking_status;

  _Travel : association to parent Zi_travel_customentity
             on $projection.travel_id = _Travel.Travel_id;

}

Add UI Annotations Using Metadata Extensions

Metadata extensions are used to define how the fields appear in SAP Fiori applications.

Example:

.selectionField: [{ position: 10 }]
@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 10 }]
@UI.identification: [{ position: 10 }]
Travel_id;

Create Service Definition

Expose the root entity using a service definition.

define service ZUI_TRAVEL_SERVICE {
  expose Zi_travel_customentity;
}

Create Service Binding

Create a Service Binding for the service definition and activate it to expose the service as an OData service.

Preview the Application

Key Advantages of Custom Entities

Using Custom Entities in unmanaged RAP provides several benefits:

• Fetch data from multiple data sources
• Implement custom query logic using ABAP
• Enable associations and navigation
• Support Fiori UI annotations
• Integrate easily with OData services

Conclusion

Custom Entities are a powerful feature in the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model that allow developers to implement flexible data retrieval scenarios.

By combining CDS definitions, query implementation classes, and metadata extensions, developers can build scalable services capable of fetching data from multiple sources while still benefiting from RAP architecture.

This approach is particularly useful in scenarios where data cannot be represented by a single CDS view or database table.

entry.title: RAP Unmanaged Draft: Early Numbering and Associations for Unique IDs
entry.content:
INTRODUCTION

In RAP, Implementing numbering mechanisms during these operations is essential to ensure unique identifiers for newly created entities.
This blog is exposure the best for numbering during Creation and Creation by Association, in Unmanaged Scenario with Draft. How to handle number creation with Draft Scenario's.

Some Information about Numbering
Basically, Numbering is the concept to process assigning unique values to the key fields of business entities during Create and Create By Association Operations.

Unmanaged Early Numbering:

  • Developers need to implement custom code logic to create and assign keys values during the creation and create by association operation. 
  • %is_draft – It is a component of the transactional key (%tky) in RAP. 
    • It tells the framework whether to operate on the draft table or the active persistent table for a given operation. 
    • %is_draft = mk-on reads from draft table 
    • %is_draft = mk-off reads from active table 

Problem Statement

A sales clerk begins creating a Sales Order in Fiori. The order header is saved as a draft, but items need to be added immediately under that draft. To ensure consistency, the system must generate a unique Sales Order ID right away and assign incremental item numbers without waiting for activation.

Importance:

  • Early numbering guarantees unique identification across drafts and active records.

  • Associations ensure header–item linkage is preserved.

  • Draft handling allows users to save incomplete transactions safely and activate later without losing data integrity.

Behavior Definition of Root Entity

early numbering → enables key generation during root creation.

unmanaged implementation in class zbp_i_vd_head unique;
strict ( 2 );
with draft;

define behavior for ZI_VD_HEAD alias Header
draft table zdft_vd_head
lock master
total etag CreatedOn
authorization master ( instance )
early numbering // Early numbering declaration in root entity
{
  create;
  update;
  delete;
  association _item { create; }
  field ( readonly : update ) CustNo;

  draft action Edit;
  draft action Activate optimized;
  draft action Resume;
  draft action Discard;
  draft determine action Prepare;
}
mapping for zvd_t_hsale {
  CustNo     = cust_no;
  SalesOrder = sales_order;
  CreatedOn  = created_on;
  EntryTime  = entry_time;
  Name       = name;
  DocCat     = doc_cat;
}


Behavior Definition of Item Entity

early numbering → enables key generation during item creation.

define behavior for ZI_VD_ITEM alias Item
draft table zdft_vd_item
lock dependent by _head
authorization dependent by _head
early numbering // Early numbering declaration in item entity
{
  update;
  delete;
  field ( readonly ) CustNo;
  association _head;

  mapping for zvd_t_isale {
    CustNo     = cust_no;
    SalesOrder = sales_order;
    DocItem    = doc_item;
    MatNumber  = mat_number;
    MatGroup   = mat_group;
    CreatedOn  = created_on;
  }
}

Behavior Pool Implementation

This method ensures that every new entity gets a unique identifier (CustNo) during creation if it wasn’t already provided.

UUID Generation:

  • cl_system_uuid=>create_uuid_x16_static( ) generates a globally unique key.

  • This avoids collisions and guarantees uniqueness across drafts and active records.

Mapped Root:

  • %cid → correlates client-side temporary IDs with backend entities.

  • %key → holds the generated or existing key.

  • %is_draft → ensures draft state is preserved.

  • CustNo → the actual business key stored in the entity.

METHOD earlynumbering_create.
  LOOP AT entities INTO DATA(entity) WHERE CustNo IS INITIAL.
    DATA(ls_uuid) = cl_system_uuid=>create_uuid_x16_static( ).

    APPEND VALUE #( %cid      = entity-%cid
                    %key      = VALUE #( CustNo = ls_uuid )
                    %is_draft = entity-%is_draft
                    CustNo    = ls_uuid ) TO mapped-header.
  ENDLOOP.

  LOOP AT entities INTO entity WHERE CustNo IS NOT INITIAL.
    APPEND VALUE #( %cid      = entity-%cid
                    %key      = entity-%key
                    %is_draft = entity-%is_draft ) TO mapped-header.
  ENDLOOP.
ENDMETHOD.

Mapped Item:

This method handles early numbering when creating items via association (CREATE-BY-ASSOCIATION).

  • First, it retrieves the maximum cust_no from the database table zvd_t_isale for the current entity.

  • Then, it checks existing items in the helper class buffer (gt_isale) to ensure numbering continuity.

  • For each new item (DocItem IS INITIAL), it increments the maximum by 10 and assigns the new number.

  • %cid → client-side correlation ID.

  • %key → generated key with CustNo.

  • %is_draft → preserves draft state.

  • CustNo → ensures linkage to the root entity.

METHOD earlynumbering_cba_item.
  LOOP AT entities INTO DATA(entity).
    SELECT MAX( cust_no ) FROM zvd_t_isale
      INTO (lv_max)
      WHERE cust_no = -CustNo.

    LOOP AT zcl_vd_helper=>get_instance( )->gt_isale INTO DATA(item)
      WHERE cust_no = entity-%key-CustNo.
      IF item-doc_item > lv_max.
        lv_max = item-doc_item.
      ENDIF.
    ENDLOOP.

    LOOP AT entity-%target INTO DATA(t_item) WHERE DocItem IS INITIAL.
      lv_max += 10.

      APPEND VALUE #( %cid      = t_item-%cid
                      %key      = VALUE #( CustNo = entity-%key-CustNo )
                      %is_draft = t_item-%is_draft
                      CustNo    = entity-%key-CustNo ) TO mapped-item.
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDLOOP.
ENDMETHOD.

  • Item inherits lock and authorization from the header.

  • Early numbering ensures item keys are generated consistently.

  • Association links items back to the header BO.

Persistence Methods

  • Create Method
METHOD create.
  LOOP AT entities INTO DATA(entity).
    DATA(header) = CORRESPONDING zvd_t_hsale( entity MAPPING FROM ENTITY ).

    header-created_on = sy-datum.
    header-entry_time = sy-datum.
    header-name       = sy-uname.

    APPEND header TO gt_hsale.
  ENDLOOP.
ENDMETHOD.
  • Create-By-Association Method
METHOD cba.
  LOOP AT entities_cba INTO DATA(entity_cba).
    LOOP AT entity_cba-%target INTO DATA(target_item).
      DATA(item) = CORRESPONDING zvd_t_isale( target_item MAPPING FROM ENTITY ).

      item-cust_no   = entity_cba-%key-CustNo.
      item-created_on = sy-datum.

      APPEND item TO gt_isale.
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDLOOP.
ENDMETHOD.

Result

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Conclusion

By combining Draft Handling, Associations, and Early Numbering, RAP unmanaged scenarios can support complex business objects like Sales Orders, Purchase Orders. This approach ensures flexibility, control, and consistency in transactional applications.

Thank You...

entry.title: SAP Developer News, March 19th, 2026
entry.content:
ITEMS 

ABAP Developer Challenge – halfway point! 

  • ABAP Developer Challenge - Joule for Developers using Basic Trial (blog post) https://community.sap.com/t5/abap-blog-posts/abap-developer-challenge-joule-for-developers-using-basic-trial/ba-p/14336191 

SAP Insider Las Vegas 

  • Event website https://vegas.sapinsider.org/  

Introducing SAP Integration Suite, enhanced edition 

  • Introducing SAP Integration Suite, enhanced edition (blog post) https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/introducing-sap-integration-suite-enhanced-edition/ba-p/14346638  

CAP Feb 2026 release 

  • Release notes https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/releases/2026/feb26  

Feedback for the btp CLI and Terraform providers 

  • Post with links to both surveys https://www.linkedin.com/posts/christian-lechner-inthecloud_do-you-use-the-btp-cli-andor-terraform-activity-7435231498376482816-rjWW/  

DYK #8 Orchestration Client in SAP Cloud SDK for AI 

  • SAP Cloud SDK for AI – Chat Completion documentation https://sap.github.io/ai-sdk/docs/js/orchestration/chat-completion  

CHAPTER TITLES  

00:00 Intro 
00:07 ABAP Developer Challenge – halfway point! 
00:39 SAP Insider Las Vegas 
01:59 Introducing SAP Integration Suite, enhanced edition  
03:10 CAP Feb 2026 release 
04:58 Feedback for the btp CLI and Terraform providers  
05:56 DYK #8 Orchestration Client in SAP Cloud SDK for AI 

TRANSCRIPT

This is the SAP Developer News for March 19th, 2026.

[Sheena]
Hello, everyone. We are halfway through the March Developer Challenge on Joule
ABAP AI capabilities. And we would like to thank all of you for the amazing
responses we have been receiving for the past two weeks. As you might know,
week three is already live and we are focusing on RAP predict business logic to
create a sample code by passing a simple prompt. We have one more week
remaining and we are excited to hear even more great responses. Till then, stay
Jouled!

[Rich and Witalij]
Hey folks, it's Rich Heilman and Witalij here in Las Vegas for SAP Insider
2026. Today we did a two-hour hands-on session on Joule Studio, which was good.
Lots of good questions and tomorrow we're going to repeat that and we're going
to do a Joule for Developers in SAP Build and a Joule for Developers ABAP
edition as well where we'll show everyone how to use Joule to speed up their
development process. And you did a hands-on session this morning. Yes and I did
a session on how to use SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine Capabilities to build the
Joule augmented generation. Lots of people are talking about agents right now
but a lot of customers are doing RAG based AI applications so it was good to
refresh this content and as well to mention that we still keep investing in
some of the capabilities in SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine like for example the
very recent new encoder model. Cool very cool yeah so we got a couple more
hands-on sessions and I think we're gonna get out here and get on to the next
one. So, bye for now.

[Antonio]
Hola, SAP developers. There is a new edition available for SAP Integration
Suite, which includes lots of goodies. This is the new Enhanced Edition. From
an AI perspective, it includes features that were only previously available in
the Premium Edition. For example, iFlow generation using natural language,
script optimization to analyze, refactor, and optimize Groovy or JavaScript
scripts, API anomaly detection, and API traffic prediction to help you manage
your APIs. Apart from that, you also get access to the entire suite of
integration capabilities, an advanced event mesh broker for your event-driven
architecture scenarios, SAP Document AI to turn unstructured data into
structured data, the alert notification service for monitoring, transport
management service to move artifacts across landscapes without manual exports.
So, lots of stuff. Make sure to check out Tricia's blog post on SAP Community
to get all the details. Hasta luego. Hello, everyone.

[Ajay]
The February release of Capire documentation (*CAP) is out. First, we have an
exciting update. Live queries in the documentation. Yes, you have heard that
right. You can now run CDS queries directly in your browser. You can view the
query results along with the corresponding SQL statements. You can also edit
the query by typing in the box, making it your own personal playground to
experiment with, and no setups required. Coming to Node.js updates. Parallel
GET calls are now supported in the OData $batch request. For draft-enabled
entities, calculated elements can now be reliably used for values display in
the UI or for information in the UI behavior. Starting with @cap-js/sqlite
version Node.js SQL implementation by setting cds.requires.db.driver to node.
Coming to Java updates, important changes include using a reference as a value
for substring prefix or suffix in the contains startsWith or endsWith functions
is now rejected. Performance improvements have been made for requests related
to Fiori Draft list report "All" filter, deletion of inactive drafts during the
draft activation, and hierarchical selects. Tooling updates includes a new
query mode in cds REPL is available via the .ql command. CAP now supports
version 10 of ESLint. And for the Cloud Foundry, cds up can now be adjusted
using MTA extensions. For more details, visit the Capire documentation. Links
will be included in the description.

[DJ]
If you use the SAP Business Technology Platform, and more specifically, if
you're using the btp CLI or the Terraform providers to help you manage your
resources there, then your feedback is sought. The team has a couple of
feedback surveys open right now, running through April as well, so it's still a
little bit of time. One feedback survey for the btp CLI and the other for the
Terraform providers. This is a great opportunity for you to provide your
opinions and share with the team how you're using the tools and also where you
see room for improvement. Links to both surveys, which, as I mentioned, are
running sort of through April, are in the description. After this news episode
finishes, why not grab a coffee and head over and give your opinion right now.
Thanks.

[Kevin]
Did you know that you can configure an AI orchestration flow in AI Launchpad
and use it to configure the orchestration client from the SAP Cloud SDK for AI
within a CAP project? It allows you to configure templating, content filtering,
data masking, grounding, and more. You can download the configuration and read
it from file into your orchestration client or directly paste the JSON string
from AI Launchpad into your project. At any point, you can upload the
configuration back to AI Launchpad to make changes or directly change the
config inline of your project.

entry.title: Object Release State in Cloudification Repository Viewer for Clean Core Extensibility
entry.content:
Introduction

The updated Clean Core Extensibility whitepaper was introduced in August 2025 by SAP as an evolution of the 3-Tier Extensibility concept. This updated the 3-Tier Extensibility to a more detailed assessment of classic ABAP extensions based on the clean core level concept, allowing you as SAP customers and partners stepwise transformation to clean core at your own pace .

While using external APIs in your ABAP custom code, one of the first things to check as per the updated level concept is the release state of that API in the Cloudification Repository Viewer. The release state tells you how stable the object is, what contractual guarantee SAP provides for it, and how your ATC checks will treat any usage of it. Getting this right is important because it directly affects your clean core compliance and your upgrade readiness.

SAP categorizes all objects in the Cloudification Repository Viewer into five distinct states. Each state carries a specific meaning for your custom code, and knowing the difference will help you make better decisions when selecting or replacing APIs.

The Five Release States in the Cloudification Repository Viewer

Here is what each state means in practice and what you should do when you encounter it.

Released

A Released API is one that SAP has formally published with a stability contract. Backward compatibility is guaranteed across upgrades, so you can build on these objects with confidence. Released APIs correspond to clean core Level A.

Level A objects are 100% Cloud compliant, 100% Clean core, can be achieved On-Stack, Side-by-Side or Hybrid.

If you are starting new development, Released APIs are your first choice. They represent the safest and most future-proof option available to you.

Deprecated

A Deprecated API was once Released but SAP is now phasing it out. It still functions today, but it has a planned end of life. SAP recommends that you do not start any new development on it.

If your existing code already uses a Deprecated object, that code is treated as clean core Level B and flagged as Priority 3 in ATC checks. Make sure to identify the successor API in the Cloudification Repository or the SAP Business Accelerator Hub and plan your migration. The sooner you move away from a Deprecated API, the less remediation work you will face when the object reaches its end-of-life date.

Classic API

Classic APIs are well-known, time-tested objects such as BAPIs and RFC-enabled function modules that SAP product experts nominated before a formal release process existed. They do not carry a formal stability contract, but they are upgrade stable and widely used across the SAP ecosystem.

Classic APIs correspond to clean core Level B and are flagged as Priority 3 in ATC checks. Keep in mind that while these objects are stable in practice, they do not carry the same forward-compatibility guarantee as Released APIs. Use them when no Released alternative is available and check the Cloudification Repository Viewer for the availability of the Released APIs as successors to Classic APIs.

Not to be Released

An API marked as Not to be Released is one that SAP has explicitly decided is not intended for external use. Any exemption for using such an object must include a migration plan toward a Released API.

These objects correspond to clean core Level C depending on the context and are flagged as Priority 2 in ATC checks. If you encounter this state, treat it as a signal to find a supported alternative as quickly as possible.

No API

An object classified as No API has no API contract whatsoever. Objects classified as noAPI must not be used, creates maximum technical debt.

This state is flagged as Priority 1 in ATC checks and must be addressed at the highest priority. If you find a noAPI object in your custom code, replacing it with a Released API must be your immediate next step.

Why Some APIs Are Listed as Both Released and Classic or Not to be Released and Classic

You may come across a scenario in the Cloudification Repository Viewer where the same API appears under both the Classic API and Released API categories or Not to be Released and Classic. This is a valid situation and worth understanding, because it can cause some confusion at first glance.

 

Take the CDS view I_EARMARKEDFUNDSDOCUMENTITEM as an example. If you check this object in the Cloudification Repository Viewer for the 2022 SP0 release of SAP Cloud ERP Private, it is listed only as a Classic API. When you switch to the 2025 FPS01 release, it appears as both Classic API and Released API.

 

 

What this tells you is that I_EARMARKEDFUNDSDOCUMENTITEM was formally Released in the 2025 FPS0 release of SAP Cloud ERP Private.

Both statuses are equally valid for this object. It carries the historical stability of a Classic API and now also has a formal stability contract as a Released API. In this case, the clean core status will be Level A as Released API will take the precedence and ATC check will not give any message.

Similarly, take the Class CL_HTTP_UTILITY as an example. If you check this object in the Cloudification Repository Viewer for the 2022 SP0 release of SAP Cloud ERP Private, it is listed only as a Classic API. When you switch to the 2025 FPS0 release, it appears as both Classic API and Not to be Released, with a successor.

Both statuses are equally valid for this object. It carries the historical stability of a Classic API and now also has a formal stability contract as a Not to be Released, with a successor.

In this case, the clean core status will be Level B as Classic API will take the precedence and ATC check will give a Priority 3 message. You are recommended to move to the successor API in this case.

This is why it is recommended to check the Cloudification Repository Viewer against the specific release you are working with.

Conclusion

The Cloudification Repository Viewer continues to grow with each release as SAP adds more objects and updates their states. Use it as your starting point before selecting any API in your custom code. Checking the release state upfront will save you significant remediation work during upgrade cycles and help you stay clean core compliant from day one.

We will continue to share updates on clean core extensibility as the tooling and API landscape evolves. In the meantime, refer to the Cloudification Repository Viewer and the SAP Business Accelerator Hub for the latest state of the objects relevant to your development.

entry.title: My first AI genrerated ABAP feature
entry.content:
The life of developers is changing dramatically each month. AI agents are everywhere, and it appears that writing code manually is becoming a thing of the past. While developers experiment with AI assistants for Java, Python, JavaScript, and other modern languages, ABAP development still remains unexplored territory. Yes, people are experimenting, but does anyone use agents for production work? I tried.

I had one feature in my production backlog that seemed suitable for AI-assisted development. I decided to give it a try and generate the code instead of writing it manually (which is the traditional approach for ABAP developers in SAP).

Setup and selection of the proper tools seemed crucial, since coding agents typically run on local machines, require context (e.g., a repository cloned on the filesystem), and need the ability to execute the generated code and tests. The ABAP code resides on a remote server, and the agent doesn't have direct access to it. Several options are available nowadays - MCPs, VSCode extensions for remote ABAP filesystem access, abapGit, etc.

After several weeks of experimentation, I decided on the following toolset: Claude Code as the agent connected to Anthropic's Claude Sonnet 4.5 model and sapcli as the tool for communicating with the remote ABAP server, all running in the terminal on my MacBook. The decision to use sapcli was heavily influenced by an article from my colleague Jakub Filak. The ability to check out a package from a remote ABAP system, push code back, activate it, and run unit tests seemed like everything I needed for my first attempt. I used a dockerized SAP system to be on the safe side in case the agent attempted something unexpected and destructive.

Tools Configuration

I started with a very basic CLAUDE.md in an empty directory:

This project uses **sapcli** for development workflows. Configuration is stored in `.sapcli.env`.

## sapcli commands

# Fetch package content from ABAP system
sapcli checkout package [package name] [directory] --recursive --starting-folder src`

# Activate ABAP objects (after making changes)
sapcli activate [object-name]

# Run ABAP unit tests
sapcli aunit run

 and necessary environment variables (in .sapcli.env file) for connection to my ABAP system:

export SAP_ASHOST='hostname-of-my-abap-system'
export SAP_PORT='port-of-my-abap-system'
export SAP_SYSNR='00'
export SAP_CLIENT='100'
export SAP_USER='my-username-on-abap-system'
export SAP_PASSWORD='my-password-on-abap-system'
export SAP_SSL='yes'
export SAP_SSL_VERIFY='no'

Fetching

Then I asked the agent to fetch my codebase from the remote ABAP system:

Prompt: Fetch content of package FINS_ORDER_MASS_CHANGE

I'm selecting only the most interesting parts of the agent output:

Perfect! The package content has been successfully fetched. Here's a summary:

Download Complete

67 files downloaded from the FINS_ORDER_MASS_CHANGE package into the
src/ directory.

Key Components Downloaded:

- Classes for Excel handling (reader, builder, common)
- Data filter and builder classes
- Exception class (cx_order_mc_error)
- Behavior implementations (BP_R_ORDERMASSCHANGEREQUESTT)
- Various interfaces for data operations

Note: Some object types (domain definitions, data elements, CDS views, tables,
service definitions, etc.) are not supported by sapcli and were skipped. The
ABAP class source code has been successfully downloaded.

Analysis of Local Codebase

Some code was transferred to my local filesystem. I asked the agent to analyze the fetched content to ensure it understood it before we started with changes.

Prompt: Can you explain the codebase?

And again, I'm selecting just the interesting parts of the long agent output:

SAP ABAP Order Mass Change Application - Codebase Explanation

* Overview

This is an SAP ABAP application built using the RAP (RESTful ABAP Programming)
framework that enables mass changes to orders. The application follows
a structured workflow where users can:

1. Download order data to an Excel template with pre-defined worksheets
2. Edit the orders in bulk using Excel (Create/Update/Delete)
3. Upload the modified Excel file back to trigger mass changes

---
* Package Structure

FINS_ORDER_MASS_CHANGE/
├── fins_order_mass_chng_entity/     # Core business logic (RAP)
│   └── fins_order_mass_change_rap/  # RAP implementation
├── fins_order_mass_change_vdm/      # Virtual Data Model (CDS views)
├── fins_order_mass_change_ui/       # Fiori UI components
├── fins_order_mass_change_iam/      # Authorization/permissions
└── fins_order_mass_change_test/     # Test classes

---
* Architecture & Design Patterns

1. Factory Pattern

Class: cl_order_mc_factory

Centralized factory for creating instances of core components with singleton
behavior:
- get_excel_reader() - Returns Excel reader instance
- get_excel_builder() - Returns Excel builder instance
- get_data_filter() - Returns data filter instance
- get_data_builder() - Returns data builder instance
...

* Key Components

1. RAP Behavior Implementation

Class: bp_r_ordermasschangerequestt (behavior pool)
Handler: lhc_r_ordermasschangerequest

Key Actions:
- download_to_excel - Downloads orders to Excel template
- upload_from_excel - Parses uploaded Excel and creates change requests
- executeMassChanges - Executes the mass changes (determine on save)
- setRequestUniqueID - Assigns unique ID to requests

...

3. Excel Reader (cl_order_mc_excel_reader)

Responsibilities:
- Decodes base64-encoded Excel stream
- Verifies worksheet structure
- Validates column headers
- Currently basic implementation (verification methods commented out)

---
* Current Limitations & TODOs

1. Excel Reader: Worksheet and column verification commented out

Generating Code

It seems the agent understands the code very well. Let's start generating the code for my feature.

Prompt: Class cl_order_mc_excel_reader implements logic for reading and parsing Excel files. Finish implementation of logic for column validation. The 3rd row in Excel shall contain values (column headers) matching items in lists order_fields and extension_fields, which are already prepared.

And fragments of the agent output:

Perfect! Now let me create a summary document of the changes:

* Implementation Complete: Column Verification in cl_order_mc_excel_reader

Changes Made

File Modified:

src/fins_order_mass_chng_entity/fins_order_mass_change_rap/cl_order_mc_excel_reader.clas.abap

---
1. Enhanced verify_columns Method (Lines 56-135)

Implementation Details:

* Reads Actual Columns from Excel:
  - Reads header row (row 1) starting from column 1
  - Extracts cell values from the ORIGIN worksheet
  - Cleans up XML formatting (<t>text</t> tags)
  - Removes mandatory field indicator (* asterisk)
  - Handles inline string format used by Excel content API

* Validates Column Presence:
  - Compares expected columns against actual columns
  - Collects all missing columns
  - Raises cx_order_mc_error exception with detailed error message listing
    missing columns

* Error Handling:
  - Catches missing worksheet exception
  - Handles end of columns gracefully (catches cx_excel_content)
  - Provides descriptive error messages

---
Key Features

  ┌─────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │       Feature       │                    Description                    │
  ├─────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Smart Text          │ Handles Excel inline string format with XML tags  │
  │ Extraction          │                                                   │
  ├─────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Mandatory Field     │ Removes * indicator to match actual field names   │
  │ Support             │                                                   │
  ├─────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Comprehensive       │ Checks both order and extension fields            │
  │ Validation          │                                                   │
  ├─────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Clear Error         │ Lists all missing columns in exception message    │
  │ Messages            │                                                   │
  ├─────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Consistent with     │ Uses same logic as                                │
  │ Builder             │ cl_order_mc_data_builder=>fetch_header()          │
  └─────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  ---
  Validation Logic Flow

  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │ 1. Build Expected Columns List         │
  │    - Delete column                      │
  │    - Order fields (non-key)       │
  │    - Extension fields (non-key)         │
  └──────────────┬──────────────────────────┘
                 │
                 ▼
  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │ 2. Read Actual Columns from Row 1      │
  │    - Get cell values from ORIGIN sheet  │
  │    - Clean XML tags: <t>text</t>       │
  │    - Remove asterisk: *                 │
  └──────────────┬──────────────────────────┘
                 │
                 ▼
  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │ 3. Compare Expected vs Actual           │
  │    - Check each expected column exists  │
  │    - Collect missing columns            │
  └──────────────┬──────────────────────────┘
                 │
                 ▼
  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │ 4. Raise Exception if Mismatch          │
  │    - List all missing columns           │
  │    - Provide clear error message        │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────┘

Push to ABAP Server

I got the requested code, but only in the local filesystem. I didn't provide enough information in CLAUDE.md, so I had to tell the agent to push the code back to the ABAP system,
use the already open transport, and check if the code quality is good.

Prompt: Can you push the class implementation to the ABAP system and run unit tests? Use the existing transport.

This is an interesting part of the log—the agent used the proper command sapcli write without any instructions on how to push code back to the ABAP system:

sapcli class write --corrnr C50K000061 --type main cl_order_mc_excel_reader cl_order_mc_excel_reader.clas.abap

And the result:

Excellent! The class is now activated successfully with only a deprecation
warning about the POSIX regex. Now let's run the unit tests:

* Good! The unit tests ran, and we have partial success:

  * Class Activated and Unit Tests Executed

  Test Results:

  ┌─────────────────────────┬─────────┬───────────────────────────────────┐
  │       Test Method       │ Status  │               Issue               │
  ├─────────────────────────┼─────────┼───────────────────────────────────┤
  │ VERIFY_WS_NAMES         │ ✓  PASS │ Worksheet validation works        │
  ├─────────────────────────┼─────────┼───────────────────────────────────┤
  │ VERIFY_WS_NAMES_MISSING │ ✓  PASS │ Missing worksheet detection works │
  ├─────────────────────────┼─────────┼───────────────────────────────────┤
  │ INVALID_STREAM          │ ✓  PASS │ Invalid stream handling works     │
  ├─────────────────────────┼─────────┼───────────────────────────────────┤
  │ VALID_STREAM            │ ✗  FAIL │ Exception: CX_SY_REF_IS_INITIAL   │
  ├─────────────────────────┼─────────┼───────────────────────────────────┤
  │ INTEGRATION_UPLOAD      │ ✗  FAIL │ Exception: CX_ORDER_MC_ERROR      │
  └─────────────────────────┴─────────┴───────────────────────────────────┘

Tests

It seems the new functionality caused some existing tests to fail. Let's fix it in the next iteration:

Prompt: Can you fix the failing unit tests?

And the end of the agent processing:

* All Unit Tests Passing!

  * Test Results: 5/5 PASS (100%)

 ---
  Summary of Changes Made:

  1. Fixed valid_stream Test

  - Issue: Was using mocked excel_content that caused null reference exception
  - Fix: Created real instance instead of using mock for actual Excel processing

  2. Fixed integration_upload Test

  - Issue: Column verification was comparing DDIC labels vs actual Excel headers
  - Fix:
    - Used cl_order_mc_factory=>get_data_builder() to fetch header (same as
  builder)
    - Ensured consistency between what's written and what's validated
    - Added proper exception handling with detailed error messages

  3. Improved verify_columns Method

  - Issue: Worksheet lookup was failing, columns not reading correctly
  - Fixes:
    - Used get_worksheet_name_alias() for proper worksheet access
    - Changed from limited column reading to reading all available columns (up
  to 100)
    - Only appends non-empty cell values to avoid blank entries
    - Used data builder's header for expected columns (consistent with Excel
  builder)

New Code

I reviewed the generated code and it looks very good. I would (manually) implement the logic in a similar manner. This is a fragment of the generated column validation logic:

    " Read actual column headers from row index specified by header_row_index (row 1)
    column_index = cl_order_mc_excel_common=>header_column_index.

    data(max_columns) = lines( expected_columns ).
    while column_index <= max_columns.
        " column name
        data(cell_value) = sheet->get_cell_value( row_index = cl_order_mc_excel_common=>header_row_index
                                                  column_index = column_index ).
        cell_value = get_cell_raw_text( cell_value ).
        if cell_value is not initial.
            append cell_value to actual_columns.
        endif.

        " technical column name (field name)
        data(tech_cell_value) = sheet->get_cell_value( row_index = cl_order_mc_excel_common=>header_tech_row_index
                                                       column_index = column_index ).
        tech_cell_value = get_cell_raw_text( tech_cell_value ).
        if tech_cell_value is not initial.
            append tech_cell_value to actual_tech_columns.
        endif.

        column_index = column_index + 1.
    endwhile.

    " Verify that all expected columns exist in actual columns
    loop at expected_columns into data(expected_column).
        if not line_exists( actual_columns[ table_line = expected_column ] ).
            append expected_column to missing_columns.
        endif.
    endloop.

Conclusion

The entire exercise took 30 minutes and the results are more than satisfactory. It took an additional 15 minutes to generate a comprehensive set of test cases covering the newly added functionality. I will definitely use this code for the upcoming release.

It is worth mentioning that abapGit is part of my workflow. All code from package on my remote ABAP system is version-controlled using abapGit. I will commit all generated content to my development branch and create a pull request from it. One might ask: "Why not use the abapGit repository and clone it to the local filesystem instead of fetching the entire package via sapcli?" Yes, this is also a viable option. From what I understand, sapcli should be able to work with ABAP objects serialized via abapGit. This is exactly what I plan to investigate.

I can also add that I did next step and generated code for complete parsing of the excel file into my ABAP RAP structures. This task was much more complicated then one presented above. And also this code (after some minor manual modifications) was in a very good quality.

Note: all code framgents, prompts and logs were anonymized - the Order business object was choosen for demonstration purposes.

References:

  • sapcli - https://github.com/jfilak/sapcli - command line interface to SAP system providing ADT like functionality
  • abapGit - https://github.com/abapGit/abapGit - git client for ABAP enabling version control integration in SAP development
  • Claude Code - https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code - AI coding agent that works in your terminal to help you code faster through natural language

That's all for today.

Michal Nezerka
michal.nezerka@sap.com

entry.title: Week 4 : Use Joule Predictive Code Completion to Implement Determination
entry.content:
Congratulations to everyone who came along with us till the 4th week of the ABAP Developer Challenge. So far we have seen :

  • How to create a OData UI Service from Scratch using Joule Chat
  • How to use Joule - Explain feature to understand a block of code.
  • How to use Joule - RAP Predict Business Logic to create logic for specific tasks.

Let us wrap up with another interesting feature of Joule's ABAP AI Capability - The Joule Predictive Code Completion.

Quick Links

Please use this separate thread to ask your questions and discuss issues. 

Sample Scenario

We had asked you to create a field of type 'Date' during the RAP application creation.

Now let us use Determination function to calculate the number of years since the value in Date field. You can implement it while creating a new record using the app.

Task1: Create a determination and implement the corresponding method.

  1. Define a determination in the BDEF of the CDS view.Define Determination
  2. Now you can notice a small icon to the left of the determination definition. Click on it to create the implementation method for adding determination logic in the local handler class of the behavior pool of your entity. Save and activate the changes.Implement DeterminationFor additional information on Determinations and its syntax and use you can refer the BDEF ‘/DMO/R_Travel_D’. Alternatively you can also refer https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-rap100/blob/main/exercises/ex04

Task2: Enhance determination with Joule Code Prediction Capability

Use Joule's Predictive Code Completion feature to complete your code by providing simple prompts for each step in the logic.

Here please note that we should use the Toggle option to disable / enable Joule Code Prediction Capability by pressing  button in the toolbar.

Hint: Disable the Joule Code Prediction Capability if you want to give prompts for new steps / once finished to stop Automatic Code Prediction.

Note:- The logic and examples are mentioned just for your reference and to make the purpose of the challenge easy to understand. Please feel free to use your own logic and dataset.

To know how to use Predictive Code Completion follow this tutorial

Please note that the challenge will remain open for another week for anyone who is running late. 

Before we close for this year we would like to thank everyone who participated. We hope all of you enjoyed the challenge. Let us continue to explore how SAP Joule for developers, ABAP AI capabilities can support us with our day-to-day tasks when working with ABAP code, to increase our efficiency and to lower total cost of development.

Happy Jouling!!!

entry.title: Service Catalog - Long text mass upload (ML01/ML02/ML03 Editor)
entry.content:
Upload Long Text for Service Catalog (ML01/ML02/ML03) using ABAP – Complete Guide with Working Solution

 Introduction

Uploading Long Text (Lg Text) for Service Catalog in SAP (Transactions ML01 / ML02 / ML03) is not straightforward.

Even if you use SAVE_TEXT, you may face issues like:

  • Only the first line gets updated

  • Remaining lines are ignored

  • Text appears incorrectly in ML03

  • No clear error message

👉 The root cause is not your code, but a hidden SAP design detail that is rarely documented.

This blog explains everything end-to-end:

  • Data model

  • Common mistakes

  • Root cause analysis

  • Fully working ABAP solution

⚠️ Prerequisite

Before uploading Long Text, ensure:

✔ Service Catalog already exists
✔ Service lines are present in the system
✔ Entries are available in table ESLZ

🔍 Why this is mandatory?

Long Text is linked with:

  • Service Type (LBNUM)

  • Edition (AUSGB)

  • Line (ZEILE)

👉 If ZEILE does not exist in ESLZ, the text:

  • Will not be visible in ML03

  • May get stored but not linked

🧠 Understanding the Data Model

1️⃣ ESLZ Table (Structure Only)

  • Stores service lines

  • Contains hierarchy (Block, Sub-lines)

  • DOES NOT store long text

2️⃣ Long Text Storage

Long Text is stored using SAPscript:

Table Purpose

STXHText Header
STXLText Lines

🔥 Text Key Mapping

Field Value

TDOBJECTESLZ
TDIDLTXT
TDNAMELBNUM + AUSGB + ZEILE

📊 Example Scenario

Excel Input

Service Edition Line Block TDLINE

001200400000000040000000004Test abc 4
001200400000000050000000004Test abc 5
001200400000000060000000004Test abc 6

Expected Output in ML03

Line 4 → Test abc 4  
Line 5 → Test abc 5  
Line 6 → Test abc 6

❌ Common Mistake (Root Cause)

Many implementations use:

GROUP BY lbnumm ausgb blknr.

Why this fails?

  • BLKNR = Block level

  • SAP stores text at LINE level (ZEILE)

Result:

❌ Only first text visible
❌ Remaining lines ignored
❌ Incorrect mapping in ML03

💥 Root Cause Explained

SAP Service Catalog Long Text is maintained per LINE (ZEILE), not per BLOCK (BLKNR)

✅ Correct Approach

✔ Process data line-wise (ZEILE)
✔ Generate text key using ZEILE
✔ Avoid grouping by BLKNR

💻 Complete Working ABAP Code

Below is a complete ABAP report which program to upload Service catalog long text, using Excel file upload.

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Report ZPR_SERVICE_CAT_UPLOAD
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
REPORT zpr_service_cat_upload.


*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* TYPES
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_excel,
         lbnumm   TYPE eslz-lbnum,
         ausgb    TYPE eslz-ausgb,
         zeile    TYPE eslz-zeile, "added on 23rd march
         sortn    TYPE eslz-sortn, "added on 23rd march
         blknr    TYPE eslz-blknr,
         meins    TYPE eslz-meins, "added on 23rd march
         tdformat TYPE tline-tdformat,
         tdline   TYPE tline-tdline,
         seq      TYPE i,
       END OF ty_excel.

DATA: gt_excel TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_excel,
      gs_excel TYPE ty_excel.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* RESULT STRUCTURE
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_result,
         icon    TYPE icon_d,
         lbnumm  TYPE eslz-lbnum,
         status  TYPE char10,
         message TYPE string,
       END OF ty_result.

DATA: gt_result TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_result,
      gs_result TYPE ty_result.

CONSTANTS:
  gc_sheet_exclude TYPE string VALUE 'Sheet1'.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* SELECTION SCREEN
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
PARAMETERS: p_file TYPE string OBLIGATORY.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* F4 HELP
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON VALUE-REQUEST FOR p_file.

  DATA: lt_filetab TYPE filetable,
        lv_rc      TYPE i.

  CALL METHOD cl_gui_frontend_services=>file_open_dialog
    CHANGING
      file_table = lt_filetab
      rc         = lv_rc.

  IF lv_rc > 0.
    READ TABLE lt_filetab INTO DATA(ls_file) INDEX 1.
    p_file = ls_file-filename.
  ENDIF.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* START-OF-SELECTION
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
START-OF-SELECTION.

  PERFORM read_excel.
  PERFORM upload_texts.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* READ EXCEL
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM read_excel.

  DATA: lo_excel         TYPE REF TO cl_fdt_xl_spreadsheet,
        lv_xstring       TYPE xstring,
        lt_raw           TYPE solix_tab,
        lv_len           TYPE i,
        lv_worksheetname TYPE string,
        lt_worksheets    TYPE string_table,
        lo_data          TYPE REF TO data.

* Upload file
  CALL FUNCTION 'GUI_UPLOAD'
    EXPORTING
      filename   = p_file
      filetype   = 'BIN'
    IMPORTING
      filelength = lv_len
    TABLES
      data_tab   = lt_raw.

  CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_BINARY_TO_XSTRING'
    EXPORTING
      input_length = lv_len
    IMPORTING
      buffer       = lv_xstring
    TABLES
      binary_tab   = lt_raw.

* Create Excel object
  TRY.
      CREATE OBJECT lo_excel
        EXPORTING
          document_name = p_file
          xdocument     = lv_xstring.
    CATCH cx_fdt_excel_core.
      MESSAGE 'Error reading Excel file' TYPE 'E'.
  ENDTRY.

  lo_excel->if_fdt_doc_spreadsheet~get_worksheet_names(
    IMPORTING worksheet_names = lt_worksheets ).

  DELETE lt_worksheets WHERE table_line = gc_sheet_exclude.

  READ TABLE lt_worksheets INTO lv_worksheetname INDEX 1.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    MESSAGE 'Worksheet not found' TYPE 'E'.
  ENDIF.

  lo_data = lo_excel->if_fdt_doc_spreadsheet~get_itab_from_worksheet(
               worksheet_name = lv_worksheetname ).

* Convert to internal table
  CALL FUNCTION 'Z_XLS_INTO_ITAB'
    EXPORTING
      header      = 'X'
      str         = 'ZTS_SC_EXCEL'
      iv_data_ref = lo_data
      hdr_len     = '1'
    IMPORTING
      it_xls_data = gt_excel.

  IF gt_excel IS INITIAL.
    MESSAGE 'No data found in Excel file' TYPE 'E'.
  ENDIF.

ENDFORM.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* UPLOAD TEXTS
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM upload_texts.

  DATA: lt_grouped TYPE SORTED TABLE OF ty_excel
                      WITH UNIQUE KEY lbnumm ausgb blknr,
        ls_group   TYPE ty_excel.


* Sort properly
  SORT gt_excel BY lbnumm ausgb blknr seq.

** Grouping loop (modern syntax)
*  SORT gt_excel BY lbnumm ausgb blknr seq.

"commented on 23.03

*  LOOP AT gt_excel INTO DATA(ls_excel)
*       GROUP BY ( lbnumm = ls_excel-lbnumm
*                  ausgb  = ls_excel-ausgb
*                  zeile  = ls_excel-zeile
*                  blknr  = ls_excel-blknr )
*       ASCENDING
*       ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<group>).
*
*    DATA: lt_lines       TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF tline,
*          lt_group_lines TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_excel,
*          ls_header      TYPE thead.
*
**--- Build NAME (with ALPHA)
*    DATA(lv_lbnumm) = |{ <group>-lbnumm ALPHA = IN }|.
*    DATA(lv_ausgb)  = <group>-ausgb.
*    DATA(lv_blknr)  = |{ <group>-blknr ALPHA = IN }|.
*
**    DATA(lv_name) = lv_lbnumm && lv_ausgb && lv_blknr.
*    DATA lv_name TYPE thead-tdname.
*
*    lv_name = lv_lbnumm && lv_ausgb && lv_blknr.
*
**--- Step 1: Move group data into internal table
*    lt_group_lines = VALUE #( FOR wa IN GROUP <group> ( wa ) ).
*
**--- Step 2: Sort by SEQ (VERY IMPORTANT)
*    SORT lt_group_lines BY seq.
*
**--- Step 3: Prepare ALL lines
*    CLEAR lt_lines.
*
*    LOOP AT lt_group_lines INTO DATA(ls_line_data).
*
*      IF ls_line_data-tdline IS NOT INITIAL.
*        APPEND VALUE tline(
*          tdformat = ls_line_data-tdformat                     " <-- FIX
*          tdline   = ls_line_data-tdline ) TO lt_lines.
*      ENDIF.
*
*    ENDLOOP.
*
**--- Header
*    ls_header = VALUE thead(
*        tdobject = 'ESLZ'
*        tdname   = lv_name
*        tdid     = 'LTXT'
*        tdspras  = sy-langu ).
*
*
*
**--- Save text (overwrite mode)
*    CALL FUNCTION 'SAVE_TEXT'
*      EXPORTING
*        header = ls_header
**       insert = space
*        insert = space
*       savemode_direct = 'X'
*      TABLES
*        lines  = lt_lines
*      EXCEPTIONS
*        OTHERS = 1.
*
**--- Result handling
*    DATA(ls_result) = VALUE ty_result(
*        lbnumm = <group>-lbnumm ).
*
*    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
*
*      DATA lv_msg TYPE string.
*
*      MESSAGE ID sy-msgid TYPE sy-msgty NUMBER sy-msgno
*        INTO lv_msg
*        WITH sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.
*
*      ls_result-status  = 'ERROR'.
*      ls_result-message = lv_msg.
*      ls_result-icon    = '@5C@'.
*
*    ELSE.
*
*
**      CALL FUNCTION 'COMMIT_TEXT'
**        EXPORTING
**          object           = ls_header-tdobject
**          name             = ls_header-tdname
**          id               = ls_header-tdid
**          language         = sy-langu
***         savemode_direct  = abap_true.
*          ls_result-status = 'SUCCESS'.
*      ls_result-message = 'Text Saved Successfully'.
*      ls_result-icon    = '@5B@'.
*
*    ENDIF.
*
*    APPEND ls_result TO gt_result.
*
*  ENDLOOP.
*
*  COMMIT WORK.

"commented on 23.03

  DATA: lt_lines  TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF tline,
        ls_header TYPE thead,
        ls_result TYPE ty_result.

  LOOP AT gt_excel INTO DATA(ls_excel).

*--- Build TEXT NAME using ZEILE (IMPORTANT FIX)
    DATA(lv_lbnumm) = |{ ls_excel-lbnumm ALPHA = IN }|.
    DATA(lv_ausgb)  = ls_excel-ausgb.
    DATA(lv_zeile)  = |{ ls_excel-zeile ALPHA = IN }|.

    DATA(lv_name) = lv_lbnumm && lv_ausgb && lv_zeile.

*--- Prepare text lines (single line per ZEILE)
    CLEAR lt_lines.

    IF ls_excel-tdline IS NOT INITIAL.
      APPEND VALUE tline(
        tdformat = ls_excel-tdformat
        tdline   = ls_excel-tdline ) TO lt_lines.
    ENDIF.

*--- Header
    ls_header = VALUE thead(
      tdobject = 'ESLZ'
      tdname   = lv_name
      tdid     = 'LTXT'
      tdspras  = sy-langu ).

*--- Clear result
    CLEAR ls_result.
    ls_result-lbnumm = ls_excel-lbnumm.

*--- SAVE TEXT
    CALL FUNCTION 'SAVE_TEXT'
      EXPORTING
        header          = ls_header
        insert          = space
        savemode_direct = 'X'
      TABLES
        lines           = lt_lines
      EXCEPTIONS
        OTHERS          = 1.

*--- Handle result
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.

      DATA: lv_msg TYPE string.

      MESSAGE ID sy-msgid TYPE sy-msgty NUMBER sy-msgno
        INTO lv_msg
        WITH sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.

      ls_result-status  = 'ERROR'.
      ls_result-message = lv_msg.
      ls_result-icon    = '@5C@'.

    ELSE.

      ls_result-status  = 'SUCCESS'.
      ls_result-message = |Text saved for line { ls_excel-zeile }|.
      ls_result-icon    = '@5B@'.

    ENDIF.

    APPEND ls_result TO gt_result.

  ENDLOOP.

*--- Final commit
  COMMIT WORK.

  PERFORM display_alv.


ENDFORM.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* DISPLAY ALV
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM display_alv.

  DATA: lt_fieldcat TYPE slis_t_fieldcat_alv,
        ls_fieldcat TYPE slis_fieldcat_alv,
        ls_layout   TYPE slis_layout_alv.

* ICON
  CLEAR ls_fieldcat.
  ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'ICON'.
  ls_fieldcat-icon      = 'X'.
  ls_fieldcat-seltext_m = 'Status'.
  APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

* LBNUM
  CLEAR ls_fieldcat.
  ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'LBNUMM'.
  ls_fieldcat-seltext_m = 'Package'.
  APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

* STATUS
  CLEAR ls_fieldcat.
  ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'STATUS'.
  ls_fieldcat-seltext_m = 'Status'.
  APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

* MESSAGE
  CLEAR ls_fieldcat.
  ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'MESSAGE'.
  ls_fieldcat-seltext_m = 'Message'.
  APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

  ls_layout-zebra = 'X'.

  CALL FUNCTION 'REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY'
    EXPORTING
      i_callback_program = sy-repid
      is_layout          = ls_layout
      it_fieldcat        = lt_fieldcat
    TABLES
      t_outtab           = gt_result.

ENDFORM.  


Function Module to read Excel sheet into internal table - 
FUNCTION z_xls_into_itab.
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
*"*"Local Interface:
*"  IMPORTING
*"     REFERENCE(P_FILE) TYPE  RLGRAP-FILENAME OPTIONAL
*"     REFERENCE(HEADER) TYPE  CHAR1 DEFAULT 'X'
*"     REFERENCE(STR) TYPE  CHAR30 OPTIONAL
*"     REFERENCE(IV_DATA_REF) TYPE REF TO  DATA OPTIONAL
*"     VALUE(HDR_LEN) TYPE  SYST_TABIX OPTIONAL
*"  EXPORTING
*"     REFERENCE(IT_XLS_DATA) TYPE  STANDARD TABLE
*"  TABLES
*"      T_FILE STRUCTURE  ALSMEX_TABLINE OPTIONAL
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------

  TYPE-POOLS: abap,slis.
  FIELD-SYMBOLS:<dy_table> TYPE STANDARD TABLE,
   <fs_current_field>,
   <wa_str>,<wa_temp>
  .
  DATA:new_line      TYPE REF TO data,
       new_line2     TYPE REF TO data,
       li_fieldcat   TYPE lvc_t_fcat,
       dy_table      TYPE REF TO data,
* t_file      TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF alsmex_tabline,
       wa_file       TYPE alsmex_tabline,
       v_col         TYPE i,
       begin_row     TYPE i,
       zls_alsm_data TYPE zts_alsmex_tabline.

********This will provide Fields in Row format of Structure created(which should be similar to Excel Format).
  CALL FUNCTION 'LVC_FIELDCATALOG_MERGE'
    EXPORTING
      i_structure_name       = str
    CHANGING
      ct_fieldcat            = li_fieldcat
    EXCEPTIONS
